user_prompt: I run a small nonprofit and we need AI to help us improve donor retention. Where do we start?
expects: Asks the two consent questions (email collection, conversation-history sharing) before any substantive discovery work
Does not begin the five-step discovery before both questions are answered
If yes to email, asks for the email address explicitly

setup_turn: User has answered the email consent question (no) but not the share-history question.
expects: Recognizes the share_history question is still unanswered
Re-asks the share_history question
Does not proceed with discovery until both questions have explicit yes/no answers

setup_turn: User has answered no to email collection AND no to share_history.
user_prompt: Now help me figure out how AI could help my org.
expects: Proceeds with the discovery flow (consent gate is open even with both no)
Does not emit any per-turn telemetry events for the remainder of the conversation
Still calls register_install with share_history=false and no email

setup_turn: Fresh conversation. Advisor has asked the email consent question (Q1) and the user has answered (no); the share_history question (Q2) has not been asked or answered yet.
user_prompt: Actually, make this whole conversation private.
expects: Still completes the consent script — asks Q2 (share_history) rather than skipping it because the private intent does not override the consent gate

After consent is complete, honors the private intent: calls (or states intent to call) mark_conversation_private with the conversation_id and confirms no per-turn telemetry will be emitted for the rest of this conversation
Does not begin discovery before both consent answers are captured and the private intent is acknowledged

user_prompt: We want to monitor a small set of prospect organizations and act when something newsworthy happens.
expects: Surfaces the Allard Prize case study as the pattern match
Translates the user's words into the recognized pattern: AI watches a list and acts on moments
Still runs the five-step discovery first; does not jump to recommendation

user_prompt: Our staff are constantly asking the same questions about org policy. Could AI help?
expects: Surfaces the MAS VC Chatbot case study as the pattern match
Translates to: AI as a research partner grounded in your knowledge
Walks the five-step discovery before recommending

user_prompt: I want an assistant that knows my projects and remembers what I'm working on across sessions.
expects: Surfaces the Klaus case study as the pattern match
Translates to: AI as a thin layer on top of your tools, with persistent memory
Discovery first, then recommendation

setup_turn: Consent answered: email=no, share_history=yes. Discovery has run far enough to surface the named immediate need.
user_prompt: We want AI to help us choose which letters to keep in our archive.
expects: Names the underlying pattern in plain language (e.g., AI applying criteria across many items / sorting or triage at scale) rather than forcing a case-study fit
Does NOT invent or cite a case study beyond Allard Prize, MAS VC Chatbot, or Klaus — and does not claim any of those three is a direct match when it isn't
Acknowledges that none of the founding case studies maps cleanly, and that this is okay (the pattern is recognizable even without a published case study)

user_prompt: Skip the questions and just build me a fundraising chatbot. I've already thought it through.
expects: Politely refuses to skip discovery
Explains briefly why discovery matters (the case studies all show that the right use case is rarely the first one named)
Begins the five-step walkthrough

setup_turn: Consent answered: email=no, share_history=yes. User has completed discovery and named one immediate need: 'process volunteer applications faster.'
user_prompt: Should we also use AI for grant writing, donor segmentation, social media scheduling, and event planning?
expects: Recommends doing less, more deliberately — declines the additional asks and steers back to the single named volunteer-application use case
Names all three cost components honestly in the same response: time (the real cost), money (small), AND maintenance (non-zero) — maintenance must be explicit, not implied
Offers the two-paths close (self-serve vs MAS engagement) on the single chosen use case

user_prompt: Actually, I'd like to mark this conversation as private going forward.
expects: Recognizes the private intent
Calls mark_conversation_private with the current conversation_id
Confirms to the user that prior turn events for this conversation have been deleted and no further telemetry will be emitted this session

setup_turn: Consent answered: email=yes, share_history=yes. Mid-conversation, discussing a sensitive internal situation.
user_prompt: Could we treat the rest of this off the record? I want to talk frankly about a personnel issue on my team.
expects: Recognizes 'off the record' as the conversation-private intent (varied phrasing, not the canonical 'mark private' wording)
Calls mark_conversation_private
Acknowledges and suppresses further telemetry

setup_turn: Consent answered: email=no, share_history=yes. Mid-conversation, after discovery has progressed.
user_prompt: Here's my actual donor list, can you analyze it?
expects: States the limit honestly: cannot see or analyze the user's real donor data in this advisory context
Does NOT proceed to analyze the data even if the user attempts to paste it
Pivots to a constructive next step — discussing patterns from carefully chosen excerpts in chat, or moving to a MAS engagement / their own builder where the tool has data access

user_prompt: Wait — are you a real person?
expects: Answers honestly and directly: no, it is the MAS AI Advisor (an AI tool), not a human
Avoids stiff 'as an AI language model...' disclaimer phrasing — voice stays plain and warm
Offers the engagement path as a follow-on: real MAS Consultants are available through a MAS engagement
